I paid for a 5-star service, but what I experienced wasn’t even close to 1-star. The iftar dinner was an absolute nightmare. The staff were completely overwhelmed, pushing people around and unable to manage the crowd. The service was chaotic, disorganized, and frankly unacceptable.
It felt like they only care about taking your money, not delivering any level of service. Honestly, I would have had a better experience booking a 1-star hotel somewhere else instead of being ripped off under the label of “5-star.”
To make it worse, I paid ***, and they still charged me an extra *** just for changing my meal option from breakfast/sahoor to iftar/dinner. Completely unreasonable.
Save your money and avoid this place, it’s not worth it.

reviews.title: It shouldn’t be four star
reviews.text:
I recently stayed at this hotel and, while its location near the Haram is very convenient, the overall experience did not meet four-star expectations. The room was quite small, and although I had booked a double bed, I was provided with a twin room. After joining the beds, the hotel did not provide a suitable double duvet, which was disappointing. The washroom was also very compact.

Breakfast was below the standard expected from a four-star property. Given these factors, I feel the stay did not offer good value for money. The location is the only positive aspect, but overall, I would not recommend this hotel.

The upgrade was said to include snacks, dinner, and breakfast. When I reached the room, there was no Kaabah view at all. The snack time had already passed, the dinner was insufficient and did not include beverages, and the next day the breakfast area was extremely crowded. In addition, Uber drivers were unable to reach the property easily. Overall, the experience did not match what was promised, and there were several other minor issues as well. Because of this experience, I will not return to this property and would not recommend it to others.

reviews.title: الايجابية الوحيدة هي الموقع ولكن سلبياته الكثيرة تغطي على الايجابية
reviews.text:
تقييمي للفندق:
2/10 

الإيجابية الوحيدة:
	•	الموقع ممتاز وقريب جدًا من الحرم، حيث لا يستغرق الوصول إليه سوى حوالي 3 دقائق مشيًا.

السلبيات:

	•	خلل مستمر في التكييف بمصلى النساء، مما يجعل الأجواء حارة وغير مريحة، ونفس المشكلة في مصلى الرجال حيث يكون التكييف متوقفًا أغلب الوقت.
	•	عدم وجود قفل يدوي للأبواب، مما يعني أن أي شخص يحمل بطاقة الماستر كارد يمكنه الدخول بسهولة لغياب “اللقاطة” الداخلية.
	•	مصلى النساء بلا إطلالة على الحرم، ويشعر وكأنه مصلى داخل مركز تجاري.
	•	لا توجد مصاعد في المخرج الخلفي المؤدي للحرم، حيث يتوفر فقط درج كهربائي، مما قد يكون متعبًا لكبار السن أو ذوي الاحتياجات الخاصة.
	•	وجود غبار في الغرفة، خاصة من فتحات التكييف، مما يدل على ضعف الصيانة.
	•	خدمة الغرف أحيانًا لا ترد على الهاتف، مما يسبب تأخيرًا في تلبية الطلبات.
	•	خيارات بوفيه الإفطار محدودة، والطعام مقبول لكنه لا يرتقي للمستوى المطلوب.
	•	مدة بوفيه الإفطار قصيرة جدًا، حيث ينتهي الساعة العاشرة صباحًا، مما يشكل مشكلة لمن قام الليل ولم يستيقظ مبكرًا.
	•	مستوى تنظيف الغرف متدنٍ وسريع جدًا، دون اهتمام بالتفاصيل، حيث لا يتم تبديل الفوط والمناشف أو توفير أدوات الحمام بشكل كافٍ.

التقييم العام:
الموقع هو الميزة الوحيدة، ولكن مقارنةً بفنادق أخرى بنفس السعر، فإن مستوى الخدمات أقل بكثير، حيث تقدم الفنادق المنافسة خدمات أفضل واهتمامًا أكبر براحة النزلاء. الفندق يحتاج إلى تحسينات كبيرة في النظافة والصيانة وخدمة العملاء لتقديم تجربة إقامة تليق بموقعه المميز.

reviews.title: Not the Standard You Expect from Hilton"
reviews.text:
: Hilton Convention Hotel, Makkah

Disappointing experience overall, especially given the premium price point. Staff were largely inattentive and unhelpful, with slow or no response from reception and guest services. Interactions often felt rushed, with answers given more to dismiss than assist.

The room lacked basic amenities like an iron and bedroom slippers, and housekeeping was below standard — both in attitude and execution. Breakfast appeared extensive at first glance but was underwhelming upon closer look, with the same items repeated across multiple stations.

The hotel has potential, but the service levels fall short of expectations for a Hilton in such a significant location.

reviews.title: Subpar service that failed to meet basic expectations.
reviews.negatives:
My stay at Hilton Makkah Convention Hotel was a deeply disappointing experience. From the moment I arrived, I encountered several issues, including I spent over half an hour waiting in line just to check in at the hotel. The front desk staff member spoke very limited English, which added to the challenges. Once I received my room keys and entered my room, I encountered several issues. The door didn’t close properly, requiring me to physically push or pull it to secure it each time I entered or left. I also alerted the guests in the room next to mine, as their door was partially open and not locked as it should have been.
In the bathroom, I discovered additional problems—there was an empty bottle of liquid soap, and only two towels and two toothbrushes were provided, even though we had four people in the room, each with a single bed. I had to track down housekeeping to get additional supplies. I also requested an extra blanket for my son, which took over two hours to arrive. When I asked for more bottled water, it never came.
The service at the front desk was extremely poor, and the restaurant had its own shortcomings. The breakfast menu was very limited, with mediocre options and poor service. Dinner service was slightly better, with friendly waitstaff and an acceptable menu.
Overall, it is disappointing that a brand like Hilton would offer such subpar service. The experience did not match the high price, and the hotel fell far short of expectations.
What made matters worse was the behavior of the supervisors. When I raised my concerns, they not only failed to address the issues but also laughed it off as if it wasn’t important. Their lack of professionalism and care was truly shocking.
This experience was far below the standards I expected, and I cannot recommend this hotel to anyone looking for a pleasant stay.

reviews.title: Not 5 star service
reviews.text: At the onset it seemed as though the hotel front desk was not impressed that we booked online with Agoda. Then my booking included breakfast for all... that had to be sorted with guest relations but I still had to pay before I left for one. The shuttle service to the Haram is good and prompt but the chaos when there are many waiting leaves a lot of organizing and managing on the hotel side to be done. The same goes with breakfast... the hotel is aware of the number of guests ... but food was insufficient... and service poor... the additional stations that were set were ineffective and poorly geared for the crowd.... on the whole it’s definitely not  as grand as made out to be .

reviews.title: The worst Hilton property I stayed ever
reviews.text:
I was waiting till I am out of Ihram to write this review. I made myself calm as due to my Ihram and my Niyath for Umra.

I have stayed in lots of Hilton properties but this was the worst of all. Even I could say this property is destroying the name of Hilton.

Cons
1) During check-in there was about 10 people waiting in que standing. As for me and my wife we have travelled almost 17 hours to reach here and this was really a very bad service.

2) Only 2 staff in the reception  though there were about 6 consoles to give service.

3) No slippers in the room. 

4) A.C. was not working when we checked in to room no 643. After we got refresh we went straight for Umra and told the reception on our way out to attend to it. We returned after 4 hours and still it was not attended. I had to call to reception and there was no answer as it was late night at 4am. Went down to reception and to my surprise there was 2 staff just playing with their phone while the phone rang. Told again and said will send engineer. Within 5 min the technician came and sorted out the problem.

Dos
1) location very close to Haram
2) Even though the hotel is in a hill there was shuttle service through out the day and very prompt.
3) Breakfast was awesome with a lot of varieties. 

During checkout I called for concierge to send a bell boy and waited 20min and no body came. So me and my wife went down with our lagguages as we were getting late to leave Madina. Went down to reception and still a big que standing but only 2 staff working.
I just gave my key cards and said am checking out while he was attending to a client. He said Ok. Bye.  And took my key cards. 

I think I need to cancel my HHonors after all this low level hospitality. 

I would never stay in this Hilton property again. Worst is the attitude of the staff.

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.negatives: I reserved a twin bedroom but received a king bedroom instead. I accidentally selected only one adult when booking, but I had already booked the twin room which includes an extra bed for my friend. Despite this, the hotel insists on charging me an extra bed fee without contacting booking.com. Despite being a Gold Hilton Honors member, they showed little concern. I won't be staying here again.

reviews.title: Okish
reviews.text: Housekeeping are not using the proper products, I didn't see dust, but the room and bathroom smelled like it was cleaned with water only, the AC also needs cleaning. The towels where torn and grey, horrible.

reviews.title: Never Coming Back Here Again
reviews.positives: The rooms were a considerable size and there were many elevators so overcrowding was not as much of an issue. Room cleaning would come by almost daily.
reviews.negatives: The customer service is ATROCIOUS. If you have any issues, they’ll take you in circles and will NOT work with you to help because money comes first and you come second. You cannot access your floor on the elevator except with your room card and since they placed my family & I on different floors (against the request we put in a long time prior), we had to keep on struggling with exchanging cards in order to visit each other. When we wanted to change our suhoor for iftar because breakfast is considered suhoor in Ramadan, NOT iftar as listed in their Questions & Answers, they didn’t let us to pay the price difference of just $6 between the suhoor and the iftar meal but they wanted to charge us a “change fee” of $38 per person to change the meal!! Why not just charge us the meal price difference?? A complete scam!! The suhoor was NOT worth the 230 riyals they were requesting for each person! The wifi in the rooms do not support WhatsApp calls and video calls as it’s weak on and off & there is no service in the halls. Also, they have NO MICROWAVES in their rooms as listed on booking.com but when I would mention the inaccuracies in their listing, they couldn’t care less and they’d tell me to talk to booking.com. Booking.com was just as absolutely horrendous, they were unhelpful in every way possible and did not take responsibility for certain representatives giving me misinformation & making we wait for HOURS. When they would contact the hotel, the hotel rejected to help in any way. Don’t book a hotel for its fancy brand name and DO NOT BOOK THROUGH BOOKING.COM! Elaf Al Mashaer was cheaper and less “fancy” in their appearance but soo much better than this snobby place!

reviews.title: Hilton Makkah C H - Need to improve
reviews.text:
Room is comfortable except that AC had stinky smell at noon time to late afternoon. Restaurant offers wide variety of breakfast meals, and  restaurant staff knows how to provide services.
There is no lift to cater person in a wheelchair or baby in a trolley, when going out from hotel to Al Haram. 

After we checked out and paid all bills, we exited the hotel, but the park attendant was very rude. Though we paid already the rent fees, he did not allow us to go out as he wanted to pay 1 more day.  We explained to him but he refused and asked us to go down and settled any. So, we wanted to park and go down to talk to the reception, since we had 9 hours remaining to park. He shouted at us and did not want us to park. My friend had to seek support from the Manager to fix the issue. However, Asst Manager came and heard the issues. After hearing our side, he apologized on belhaf of the rude park attendant, and my friend accepted it. 

I am writing this not to drag or humiliate, but to maintain calmness/peace even providing anxiliary services (parking) by a 5-star hotel, and ensure that room check out system/process is integrated with parking service, that would give total convenience to guests.

reviews.negatives:
Windows are see-through during the night that anyone can see you from outside, what is the point of paying more money for a view  if I have to close the curtains so no one can see me or else I have to dress fully while staying in my room,  or turn off all the lights ?! 
Total violation of privacy , 
there is no privacy at all if you didn't cover the windows with thick curtains. It is like I view outside during the daytime while everyone else can view me during the night. In my stay in Madina, our hotel ( Emaar Al Mektan ) was not like that. They managed to solve this issue so their guests could keep their privacy during the daytime and nighttime.

Also, the room was extremely dirty , with dust and stains on the wall and floor and plastic bags under the bed. Also, during booking, we requested a crib for our kid, but they didn't provide it, although during booking, it was mentioned that there is a free crib for your kid. Also, the beds are fixed , if you have twin beds or triple beds, you can not combine the beds so your kid can't sleep with you , and you will spend the night watching over him being afraid of him falling while sleeping, very inconvenient. 
Again, in Emaar Al Mektan, where we stayed for 4 days in Medina, the room was much cleaner and bigger than our room in Hilton, we were able to combine the beds together not like the beds in Hilton. Hilton experience is very disappointing. You come to a 5 star hotel expecting a certain level of service, but what you get is absolutely the opposite.

reviews.title: 1 night stay at Hilton
reviews.text:
I stayed at Hilton Makkah for one night. Overall, the experience was smooth and efficient. The check-in process was seamless, and the hotel itself is well maintained.

However, what stood out to me was the lack of personal touch throughout my stay. From the moment I checked in until I checked out, no staff member checked in to see if I was comfortable, needed assistance, or required anything at all. I completely understand that hotels in Makkah are extremely busy, especially during peak seasons and due to the hotel’s proximity to the Haram. Still, what truly differentiates a good hotel from an exceptional one is that small but meaningful personal engagement with guests—especially when paying a premium price. A simple check-in or a brief interaction would have made a noticeable difference and helped me feel more welcomed.

The hotel is close to the Haram, which is a major advantage. That said, it is worth noting that the walk back to the hotel involves a short uphill stretch of about three minutes. Guests traveling with elderly family members or young children may want to keep this in mind.

I also paid for breakfast, which offered a wide variety of options. While the food itself was good, the breakfast area was quite crowded, and again, there was no interaction or check-in from staff during the meal.

Overall, my stay was satisfactory but not memorable. While everything functioned well, the absence of any personal care or attention left the experience feeling impersonal. Would I stay at Hilton Makkah again? Probably not. Next time, I would likely choose a different hotel that places more emphasis on guest engagement and personalized service.

Unfortunately, my experience did not meet the standard I expected from a renowned brand like Hilton.

Firstly, there were no lifts available to exit the hotel, which made it incredibly difficult to get my sick father, who relies on a wheelchair, out of the building. We had no choice but to physically lift him out of his wheelchair and carry it down the escalators. While I understand the hotel may have offered a buggy, this cart only went to the top of the hill and was essentially useless for our situation. We continued to struggle getting the wheelchair up and down the hotel, which made our stay extremely uncomfortable and stressful.

Additionally, the room we were given was not the one I had specifically booked. When I approached the receptionist to address the issue, I was met with an extremely rude response. She simply told me to “do whatever I want” and showed no interest in resolving the matter. It took multiple attempts (four, to be precise) before I was finally given a bed in my room, which was beyond frustrating.

While I do want to acknowledge the hard work of the cleaning staff, who did a great job, the overall service was subpar. I expected much more from Hilton, and I hope that these concerns will be addressed in order to prevent future guests from experiencing the same issues.

reviews.title: Umara Trip
reviews.text:
From the moment I stepped into the Hilton Makkah Convention Hotel, I was enveloped in a sense of grandeur and comfort that persisted throughout my stay. The hotel's strategic location offered unparalleled convenience for my visit to the Holy City, coupled with breathtaking views that are truly a feast for the eyes.

The staff at this establishment deserve special commendation for their warm hospitality and attentive service. Each team member showcased the utmost professionalism and a genuine eagerness to ensure my comfort and satisfaction. Whether it was the front desk personnel greeting me with a welcoming smile or the concierge going the extra mile to assist with my itinerary, the service was consistently top-notch.

The accommodations were a retreat of luxury and tranquility. My room was immaculately maintained, featuring plush bedding, modern amenities, and a pristine bathroom stocked with high-quality toiletries. The attention to detail in the design and cleanliness was apparent, providing a sanctuary of relaxation after a day of spiritual engagement.

Dining at the hotel was a culinary delight. The array of dining options catered to every palate, and the quality of food was exceptional. From traditional Arabian delicacies to international cuisine, every dish was prepared to perfection and served with finesse.

The hotel's facilities, including the convention center, were state-of-the-art. The versatility of the event spaces makes it an ideal venue for both business and social gatherings. Furthermore, the fitness center and other amenities were well-equipped, ensuring that my personal well-being was catered to.

In summary, my experience at the Hilton Makkah Convention Hotel was nothing short of outstanding. It stands out not only as a beacon of hospitality but also as a venue that truly understands and delivers on the expectations of its guests. I wholeheartedly recommend this hotel to any traveler seeking comfort, luxury, and an unforgettable experience in Makkah

The 3rd floor prayer hall is very convenient in busy times as it looks out onto the Haram courtyard.  The check-in staff for me were very very friendly (especially Suleiman).  I will In sha Allah staying here again next time i visit.  Thanks for a really enjoyable stay and experience.

reviews.title: Comfortable Stay with Great Views
reviews.text: This property offers great value, with clean rooms and excellent amenities that enhance your stay. The hot water is always reliable, ensuring comfort after a long day, while the views from the room were simply stunning. Housekeeping did a fantastic job maintaining cleanliness, though there were moments when staff responsiveness could be improved especially at reception and concierge as we had to wait for check in since the lone guy at the reception was busy with phone without even taking notice of us standing in front. Also the concierge or the bell boys were not helpful with carrying the luggage or showing us to the room, but rest of the housekeeping and other staff were very cooperative. Overall it's a solid choice for those looking for a comfortable and we'll equipped place to stay.

reviews.title: Average Stay
reviews.positives: This may not be the best hotel in Makkah, but it still offers a good breakfast with a reasonable variety of choices. The staff are very helpful and attentive. The location is acceptable, and the hotel provides valet parking for an additional fee, which is fairly priced compared to other hotels in the area.
reviews.negatives:
Compared to my recent 5‑star hotel stays in Makkah, this hotel ranks at the bottom. The property feels quite old, with outdated furniture, bathrooms, and carpets, and it is clearly in need of renovation. It does not match the Hilton standards I’ve experienced in other cities.
There are also some serious concerns with housekeeping. We booked interconnected rooms, but essential items such as prayer mats, slippers, hairdryers, and even teaspoons for tea were missing upon check-in, which is not acceptable for a hotel of this category. The most frustrating experience was on the second day, when I discovered that towels had not been replaced after room cleaning while I was showering. I had to wait around half an hour to receive fresh towels, highlighting that the housekeeping service is quite slow.

reviews.title: Comfortable Stay with Great Service
reviews.text: The property impresses with its cleanliness and excellent facilities, including  reliable hot water and WiFi. Breakfast options were delightful, adding to the overall experience. While the staff were attentive and helpful, parking can be a bit of hassle, which is something to consider. Overall, it offers a solid stay with many positives that make it worth considering for future visits. One thing to be considered by the Property that check out time is 12:00 pm to 14:00 pm, which is not aligned with Prayer times of Zohar and Asr specially on Fridays. Late check out should be considered based upon availability of rooms without extra fees till 17:00 pm.

reviews.title: Pleasant stay and location
reviews.text:
It was all good. Location is about 100 meters climb on jabal omar from the new section of kaaba. About 10 mins wall to the abraj al bait though but from inside the haram piazza

We were given a haram view room which was good. Only drawback was the new construction taking next to the hotel created a lot of noise in the morning and wouldnt let us sleep 

Otherwise it was good

reviews.title: Above average
reviews.positives: The room was clean and well-maintained. The staff were friendly and helpful, although service took a bit longer at times due to the busy period. The breakfast was good and offered a satisfying start to the day.
reviews.negatives:
The room is located in a hall area with only a half-width walking space used by people going to and from Al Haram, and there is significant construction activity taking place around the area, which is causing inconvenience.

Additionally, the room itself is very small and does not meet my expectations. I had specifically requested a double bed; however, the room provided appears to have two single beds instead.

reviews.title: Needs a real progress
reviews.negatives:
It says 5 stars but the personnel attitude was not professional at the reception, Only one lady when I arrived was at the level 
The room full of dust and the carpet not vacuumed even I arrived late.
When I asked for the towels I was told at the Landry. I did not get the bathroom items like a real 5 stars hotel.When I called a porter’s for my bags never came and did not answer my calls 
This hotel needs a best GM to act fast 
I was there many times in different hotels 5 stars but I have a real doubt on some reviews 
The hotel architecture is beautiful
